LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT HE COMPLIED WITH OR IS EXCUSED FROM COMPLYING WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. SEE SHIRK V. VISTA UNIFIED SCH. DIST. (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 201, 209. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS SENT BY EMAIL. JUDGE: MARLA J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JULIE PECK, DIMITRIA STATHOPOULOS, TERESA GUILLEN AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINTR IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE ONE LAST OPPORTUNITY TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING HE IS EXCUSED FROM COMPLYING WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. (SEE SHIRK V. VISTA UNIFIED SCH. DIST. 42 CAL.4TH 201, 209 (2007).) PLAINTIFF HAS CHECKED BOX 9B ON THE FORM SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT HE IS "REQUIRED TO COMPLY WITH A CLAIMS STATUTE AND IS EXCUSED FROM COMPLYING." PLAINTIFF REFERS TO AN "ATTACHED DECLARATION." HOWEVER, THE DECLARATION DOES NOT SET FORTH FACTS AS TO WHY PLAINTIFF IS EXCUSED FROM COMPLYING; IT MERELY CITES THE SHIRK CASE AND THE LAW. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK CATO, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELIZABETH PEDERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANTS JULIE PECK, DIMITRIA STATHOPOULOS, TERESA GUILLEN AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS JULIE PECK, DIMITRIA STATHOPOULOS, TERESA GUILLEN and CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINTr is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave one last opportunity to plead facts showing he is excused from complying with the Government Claims Act. (See Shirk v. Vista Unified Sch. Dist. 42 Cal.4th 201, 209 (2007).) Plaintiff has checked box 9B on the form Second Amended Complaint that he is "required to comply with a claims statute and is excused from complying." Plaintiff refers to an "attached declaration." However, the declaration does not set forth facts as to why Plaintiff is excused from complying; it merely cites the Shirk case and the law. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Cato, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff did not give proper notice that he will appear at the hearing. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts showing that he complied with or is excused from complying with the Government Claims Act. See Shirk v. Vista Unified Sch. Dist. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 201, 209. Plaintiff is granted 30 days leave to amend the complaint. Order signed the order that was sent by email. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Motion For Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MARK CATO's Motion For Judgment is denied. The motion appears to be seeking a default and a default judgment. Department 302 does not enter defaults and default judgments. In addition, service of the summons and complaint at on Defendant at Fox Plaza is improper under CCP 416.50(a). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
